headline: SVP "No to 10 Million" immigration cap polling at 52% ahead of June 14 vote.
summary: A Tamedia survey released May 4 shows majority voter support for the first time; business groups (including Migros CEO Mario Irminger) warn that a Yes would violate EU free-movement rules and destabilise the Bilaterals III package currently before parliament.

headline: Bilaterals III in parliamentary committee phase; floor vote not before 2027–28.
summary: Following the Federal Council's March 13 dispatch, committees are working through 36 legislative amendments.

headline: Zurich Airport posts first month above 2019 pre-pandemic baseline.
summary: April 2026 recorded 23,307 movements (+5.2% YoY), finally clearing the Covid-era deficit; cargo volumes also at post-pandemic highs.

headline: Swiss April manufacturing PMI 54.5 vs. 52.0 expected.
summary: The surprise beat signals the strongest factory expansion since early 2024; export orders and employment sub-indices both in growth territory, supporting CHF strength narrative.

headline: NDB opens Josef Mengele intelligence dossier to researchers.
summary: The Swiss federal intelligence service granted academic access following a formal archival review, the latest in a series of post-war intelligence disclosures linked to Switzerland's wartime-era historical reckoning.

headline: Russia and Ukraine declare rival ceasefires ahead of Victory Day.
summary: Russia's Ministry of Defence announced a unilateral May 8–9 truce; Zelenskyy—having received no formal notice—counter-announced a Ukrainian ceasefire from midnight May 5, describing the move as proof Russia "cannot afford military equipment." Ukraine documented 400+ Russian violations during the Eas

headline: Gaza phase-2 negotiations deadlocked over Hamas disarmament.
summary: Director-General Mladenov's talks with Hamas are at impasse: Hamas refuses to disarm before Israel fully withdraws and statehood guarantees are in place; Israeli airstrikes continue, drawing a UN Security Council open-debate statement urging consolidation of the ceasefire.

headline: US-China senior calls on Taiwan and trade (Apr 30) ahead of possible Trump-Xi summit.
summary: Treasury's Bessent and Secretary Rubio spoke with Chinese counterparts on arms sales, semiconductor controls, and tariffs; separately, the PLA deployed warships near the Philippines in apparent response to the US-Japan-Philippines Balikatan 2026 exercises, signalling sustained grey-zone pressure.

headline: Pakistan marks first anniversary of the 2025 India-Pakistan conflict.
summary: The Karachi state Marka-i-Haq event (May 4) commemorates "Operation Sindoor" and the four-day aerial exchange; India's suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty remains in force, adding a structural economic dimension to the otherwise-holding ceasefire.

headline: Oxford demonstrates first-ever "quadsqueezing" quantum interaction, achieved 100× faster than expected.
summary: Using a single trapped ion and two non-commuting applied forces (exploiting non-commutativity to amplify each other), the team generated a fourth-order squeezing effect that had been theoretically accessible but experimentally elusive; the technique opens paths for quantum simulation and sensing.

headline: Sox9 overexpression in astrocytes clears Alzheimer's amyloid plaques and preserves cognition in mouse models.
summary: Baylor/WashU researchers found Sox9 activates the MEGF10 phagocytic receptor, driving direct Aβ plaque clearance; Sox9 knockout worsened plaque burden.

headline: [2605.00347](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00347)
summary: · submitted 2026-05-01 · _Odysseus: Scaling VLMs to 100+ Turn Decision-Making in Games via Reinforcement Learning_ (Shi, Li et al., Princeton) — First systematic study of long-horizon RL for VLMs: a PPO variant with turn-level critic enables VLMs to solve 100+ step Super Mario Land sequences, achiev

headline: [2605.00425](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00425)
summary: · submitted 2026-05-01 · _AEM: Adaptive Entropy Modulation for Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning_ — Supervision-free credit assignment for multi-turn agent RL via adaptive entropy modulation; addresses the sparse outcome-only reward problem without per-step labelling.

headline: [2605.00817](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00817)
summary: · submitted 2026-05-01 · _When LLMs Stop Following Steps: A Diagnostic Study of Procedural Execution in Language Models_ — Across 14 models and 55 datasets, first-answer accuracy collapses from 61% on 5-step procedures to 20% on 95-step procedures; quantifies a systematic, underexplored failure mode

headline: [2605.00380](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00380)
summary: · submitted 2026-05-01 · _ResRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Negative Sample Projection Residual Reinforcement Learning_ — Addresses generation-diversity collapse in verifiable-reward RL by projecting negative-sample gradients into residual space; improves reasoning diversity without sacrificing accu

headline: [2605.00199](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00199)
summary: · submitted 2026-05-01 · _RSAT: Structured Attribution Makes Small Language Models Faithful Table Reasoners_ — Trains 1–8B SLMs to produce step-by-step reasoning with cell-level citations via GRPO; NLI-based faithfulness reward substantially reduces hallucination on structured-data QA.

headline: Asian close (May 4):
summary: South Korea Kospi +5.12% to 6,936.99 (fresh record, extending April's historic monthly rally); Hang Seng +1.26%; ASX 200 −0.37% to 8,697.10.

headline: US futures lightly positive at Asia open; oil elevated.
